P0.title: Process technology audit & IP scope definition
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Review TTP's process capability data package: device specs, module-level performance, process window summary
Map all 142 IP items against TTP's proposed deliverable list — identify gaps in T1 (Must Transfer) category
Define process engineering training plan: which modules, which tools, which TTP process engineers are the knowledge holders
Contribute technical input to TTA negotiation: warranty on process performance, transfer session scope, wafer lot delivery requirements
Define acceptance criteria for process recipe deliverables: what constitutes 'complete' for each module recipe

P1a.title: Process recipe deliverable audit & on-site training at TTP
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive process recipe packages per module (litho, etch, CVD, PVD, ALD, CMP, implant, RTA) and log in DMS
Audit each recipe for completeness: tool model compatibility, parameter ranges, process window limits, safety flags
Conduct on-site training at TTP: observe running production for each module; capture tacit knowledge from TTP process engineers
Identify tool-set differences (Green Fab vs. TTP equipment) and co-develop adaptation recipes with TTP process team
Complete IP Completeness Audit Report for all process recipe categories

P1b.title: Equipment process recipe co-development & FAT participation
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Review TTP's transferred process recipes and identify tool-specific parameters to verify at FAT
Co-develop with key equipment vendors (etch, CVD, litho) the initial recipe set matching TTP's process conditions
Attend FAT for critical process tools: verify process performance on vendor test wafers against TTP's specification targets
Document process recipe adaptation requirements: parameters needing adjustment due to tool model differences
Issue preliminary SPC control limits based on TTP's transferred statistic limits

P1c.title: Process area utility & cleanroom class specification
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Define gas purity specifications per process module (N2 < 1 ppb O2, SiH4 ≥ 99.9999%, etc.) aligned with TTP's transferred material selection table
Specify chemical grades and delivery concentrations for each process module (HF, H2O2, TMAH, NH4OH, IPA)
Define cleanroom class requirements per zone (ISO 3 for litho bays, ISO 4-5 for general process areas)
Specify temperature (±0.1°C at litho), humidity (±1% RH), and vibration tolerances per process area
Define contamination zone boundaries and cross-contamination prevention rules

P2.title: Test vehicle execution, process recipe optimization & yield learning (target 60%)
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Execute test vehicle full-flow wafer runs (minimum 5 lots × 25 wafers): substrate to final metal passivation
Collect PCM/WAT electrical data on all device structures and compare against TTP's transferred specification targets
Run split-lot DOE on critical yield-limiting modules: identify top-3 yield limiters from electrical and defect data
Optimize process recipes module by module: adjust parameters within transferred process window to match TTP's silicon targets at Green Fab's specific tools
Build defect root-cause library: classify ≥ 80% of observed defect modes by root cause (contamination, process recipe, equipment)

P3.title: Production recipe lock-down & yield optimization for customer product (target 80%)
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Supply clean, fully controlled wafer lots for reliability qualification (zero experiments, locked recipes)
Lock all production module recipes and issue formal process specification documents with revision control
Drive customer product yield from test vehicle baseline to ≥ 80%: focus split-lot analysis on customer layout-specific yield limiters
Support integration team with process-level root cause analysis for customer product yield issues
Prepare process sign-off package for PDK v1.0: Cpk data for all critical electrical parameters, process stability report

P0.title: Integration flow review & yield target definition
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Review TTP's process integration flow document: step sequence, Q-time specs, cross-module interface requirements, process window summary
Define yield milestone targets: P2 exit ≥ 60% functional yield on test vehicle; P3 exit ≥ 80% on customer product
Map integration IP items against the 142-IP scope: identify which integration IPs are in T1 (Must Transfer) category
Define the test vehicle design scope: what test structures, what product representative circuits, what electrical characterization vehicle
Contribute to TTA terms on integration methodology transfer: training sessions required, silicon data delivery, model correlation data

P1a.title: Integration flow deliverable audit, co-development & training
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive integration flow document and audit: all process steps accounted for, Q-time specifications documented, cross-module compatibility rules complete
Co-design test vehicle with TTP integration team: select process module evaluation structures, integration test structures, yield detractor identification structures
Conduct on-site training at TTP: observe full-flow wafer runs, understand module interaction effects, review TTP's yield learning history
Audit process corner definition methodology: verify SS/TT/FF/SF/FS corner definitions match TTP's transferred SPICE models
Document all integration knowledge gaps and escalate gaps in T1 IPs to technology transfer project manager

P1b.title: Integration automation interface & SECS/GEM requirements for tools
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Define SECS/GEM data collection variable (DCV) requirements for integration-critical parameters: temperature, pressure, time, endpoint detection
Specify Q-time enforcement logic for MES: which step pairs require Q-time constraints, what actions to take on violation
Review integration requirements during equipment vendor co-development: verify tool capabilities match integration needs
Attend FAT for critical integration tools: verify process performance matches integration-level requirements
Define AMHS routing rules that respect integration Q-time constraints

P1c.title: Process flow routing & cleanroom layout integration review
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Review cleanroom layout drawings against process integration flow: verify tool placement sequence aligns with wafer flow
Identify Q-time risk zones: modules that must be physically close to avoid Q-time violations (e.g., CMP → next litho)
Define contamination zone boundaries: metal contamination zones, photoresist areas, chemical wet process areas, implant areas
Review AMHS track layout: verify automated wafer transport routes support integration flow without cross-contamination
Provide sign-off on cleanroom layout from integration perspective before construction completion

P2.title: Integration flow qualification, process corner definition & PDK v0.9 data delivery
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Design and execute integration split-lot DOE: vary critical integration parameters (gate oxide thickness, anneal budget, implant dose) to map electrical response
Characterize module interaction effects: pattern density loading on etch rate, STI stress on Vth, CMP dishing on resistance
Define 6 process corners (SS/TT/FF/SF/FS at -40°C/27°C/125°C) based on Green Fab silicon data — provide to N3 for model generation
Correlate WAT electrical data with TCAD predictions and TTP's transferred model correlations — identify deviations
Define and lock process window: approved recipe parameter ranges within which production must operate

P3.title: Production integration lock-down, customer ramp support & HVM readiness
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Freeze production integration flow specification (version-controlled, change-controlled): no module changes without PCN process
Implement formal PCN (Process Change Notification) procedure: evaluate impact, approve, implement, verify, customer notification
Develop customer ramp playbook: yield learning sequence, inline monitoring strategy, split-lot DOE protocol for new products
Support customer product yield ramp: provide integration-level analysis of customer layout-induced yield issues
Complete integration qualification report: all modules and integration interfaces qualified; sign off for PDK v1.0 release

P0.title: PDK scope definition, IP portfolio review & EDA infrastructure planning
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Define PDK v0.9 and v1.0 scope: target device types, process corners, EDA tool suite (Cadence/Synopsys/Siemens), hardened IP list
Review TTP's PDK deliverables list against 142-IP scope: identify all T1 PDK items and verify they are included in TTP's transfer package
Plan EDA infrastructure: license server capacity, compute cluster for SPICE simulation, Git-based PDK version control, customer data exchange portal
Define PDK development timeline: aligned with test vehicle wafer availability (P2) → PDK v0.9 release → customer tape-out
Contribute to TTA PDK terms: protocol compliance standard, PDK format, SPICE model format (BSIM4/PSP), EDA tool compatibility requirements

P1a.title: PDK deliverable audit, EDA setup & co-development with TTP PDK team
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive and audit SPICE model package: BSIM4 parameter file, corner model set, statistical model, Verilog-A models — verify all device types covered
Audit DRC/LVS/PEX rule deck completeness: run on TTP's reference test chip layout, verify 0 DRC errors on clean reference design
Receive and audit standard cell library: timing (.lib), layout (GDSII), LVS netlist (.cdl), LEF — verify all drive strength variants present
Set up EDA environment at Green Fab: Cadence Virtuoso, Synopsys HSPICE, Siemens Calibre — configure license servers, PDK directory structure, version control
Co-design PCM test structure wafer with TTP PDK team: OPC calibration targets, SPICE model extraction structures, DRC validation patterns

P1b.title: Litho & CD measurement tool FAT — PDK characterization capability
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Attend FAT for DUV exposure tool: verify CD uniformity (3σ < 2%) and overlay (< 5 nm 3σ) at Green Fab's target test patterns
Attend FAT for CD-SEM: verify measurement repeatability σ < 0.3 nm on PDK calibration patterns
Review litho tool recipe management: verify PDK test chip and OPC calibration wafer recipes can be managed and downloaded
Document tool fingerprint data (lens aberration, scan calibration) as PDK baseline for future OPC model calibration
Verify mask data preparation interface compatibility with Green Fab's PDK tape-out flow

P1c.title: EDA server room, license infrastructure & PDK data security setup
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Define EDA server room specifications: rack requirements, power capacity (UPS), cooling, network connectivity to cleanroom and customer portal
Deploy Git-based PDK version control system and CI/CD build pipeline for automated PDK release generation
Set up customer PDK portal with NDA-gated access: customer authentication, download logging, version tracking
Implement PDK export control compliance: ECCN 3E001 classification review; Technology Control Plan (TCP) for controlled PDK elements
Configure secure EDA license server with FlexLM/RLM and configure Cadence/Synopsys/Siemens license counts

P2.title: SPICE model calibration, DRC/LVS validation & PDK v0.9 release
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Calibrate BSIM4 SPICE parameters from Green Fab test vehicle silicon: fit Vth0, Ids, Cgs, Cgd vs. WAT data — target Ids error < ±5% across all bias points
Generate 6-corner model set (SS/TT/FF/SF/FS at -40°C/27°C/125°C) using process corner definition from N2; validate corners against WAT distribution
Run Calibre DRC on customer test chip layout using Green Fab DRC rules — achieve 0 errors before tape-out
Run Calibre LVS on customer test chip schematic vs. extracted layout — achieve 0 mismatches
Characterize standard cell timing and power (Liberty .lib) from Green Fab process silicon data; release PDK v0.9 package to first customer

P3.title: PDK v1.0 release, customer tape-out support & DFY guidelines
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Release PDK v1.0: production SPICE/corner models (updated from P3 silicon), full DRC/LVS/PEX rule decks, complete standard cell library, SRAM compiler, ESD clamps/pad rings, IO libraries
Support concurrent customer tape-outs: respond to DRC/LVS signoff queries, model accuracy questions — within 24-hour SLA
Develop DFY guidelines from P2/P3 yield learning: antenna ratio recommendations, density fill strategy, pattern-specific yield rules
Establish PDK versioning and maintenance process: minor (v1.x) vs. major (v2.0) revision policy; customer notification protocol
Begin characterization of additional hardened IP (analog, mixed-signal, eFuse) for PDK v1.1 roadmap

P0.title: Equipment vendor evaluation, spec development & procurement strategy
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Develop detailed equipment specifications for all process tool types based on TTP's transferred equipment configuration data and material selection table
Evaluate 2-3 vendors per tool type: benchmark against TTP's transferred process requirements, assess delivery lead time and FSE support capability
Define FAT and SAT acceptance protocols for each tool type: test items, acceptance criteria, pass/fail conditions, punch-list process
Plan equipment delivery sequence and cleanroom installation choreography aligned with P1c construction timeline
Define spare parts strategy: critical spares list, reorder points, on-site stock vs. vendor-managed inventory

P1a.title: Equipment maintenance IP audit & tool-specific knowledge transfer
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive and audit TTP's equipment maintenance IP package: PM procedures, spare parts BOM per tool type, process-specific calibration intervals
Identify tool types where TTP's tools differ from Green Fab's purchased tools — develop adaptation plan for PM procedures
Conduct training at TTP: shadow TTP equipment engineers for critical tool types (etch chambers, litho scanner, CMP); observe PM procedures
Audit TTP's FDC rule framework: understand which sensor parameters were used for fault detection in each tool category
Receive TTP's tool-to-tool matching criteria: understand what 'matched' means for each tool type

P1b.title: FAT execution lead, equipment installation, hook-up & SAT
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Attend FAT at each vendor site: verify hardware configuration, test safety interlocks, measure utility consumption vs. spec, sign FAT protocol
Supervise cleanroom move-in: position tools on vibration isolation pads (±0.5 mm flatness), execute utility hook-up checklist at each connection point
Execute initial power-on sequence per vendor IQ (Installation Qualification) procedure; test all safety interlocks in Green Fab environment
Execute SAT protocol: repeat all FAT tests in Green Fab utility environment; sign SAT acceptance certificate; initiate warranty period and first PM cycle
Establish PM program in CMMS: define PM intervals, parts BOM, procedure SOP for each tool type based on TTP's transferred PM IP adapted to Green Fab's tools

P1c.title: Equipment utility specifications & sub-fab design input
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Compile utility requirement matrix per tool type: power (kVA, phase), UPW (flow rate, resistivity), gases (purity, pressure, flow), chemicals, exhaust, abatement
Design sub-fab layout: utility stub positions, chemical mechanical interface panels, exhaust duct routing for each tool position
Specify vibration isolation requirements per tool type: floor vibration spectrum limits for litho tools (< 1 µm/s), CD-SEM tools, metrology tools
Verify planned cleanroom floor space against equipment footprints — flag any layout conflicts before construction lock-in
Define equipment delivery access routes through the facility and coordinate with N8 (Facility) on construction sequencing

P2.title: Tool qualification, FDC deployment & tool-to-tool matching program
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Execute tool qualification procedure for each tool: MFC calibration (±1%), pressure controller, temperature sensor, RF power — verify against TTP's transferred calibration specs
Run tool-to-tool matching qualification: process identical wafers across all same-type tools, verify results within TTP's transferred matching criteria
Deploy FDC rules from TTP's transferred framework, adapted to Green Fab's specific tool models and sensor sets — tune thresholds on actual process data
Achieve SEMI E10 equipment availability baseline > 80% for all critical tools before P2 yield target achievement
Complete operator/technician training: certify independent operation for all tool types; document training records in QMS

P3.title: HVM equipment effectiveness, predictive maintenance & capacity planning
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Achieve SEMI E10 equipment availability target ≥ 85% for all critical-path process tools before HVM commitment
Deploy predictive maintenance models: use P2 FDC trend data to predict PM needs before performance degradation — reduce unplanned downtime
Establish 24/7 on-call equipment escalation process: primary → secondary → vendor FSE escalation with defined response time commitments
Optimize spare parts inventory: set reorder points based on P2 consumption rates; negotiate consignment stock with key vendors for critical parts
Plan capacity expansion tool procurement: forecast additional tool count from HVM wafer start targets; issue RFQ 18 months before needed

P0.title: Metrology strategy definition & tool specification for procurement
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Review TTP's transferred measurement definitions: which parameters to measure, at which process steps, with which measurement tools — assess completeness
Define metrology tool requirements for all measurement types: CD-SEM, overlay metrology, film thickness (XRF, ellipsometry), sheet resistance, defect inspection
Specify environmental requirements for metrology bays: vibration (< 1 µm/s for CD-SEM), temperature stability (±0.5°C), EMI shielding
Plan NIST-traceable calibration reference standard management: which standards, refresh frequency, storage conditions
Define inter-tool matching strategy: how many tools of each type, matching protocol, acceptable inter-tool offset

P1a.title: Metrology methodology audit & measurement system capability training
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive and audit metrology information package: measurement recipe structures, tool assignment logic, measurement frequency targets per process step
Audit TTP's measurement-to-yield correlation data: verify which inline parameters have highest predictive power for final electrical yield
Conduct training at TTP metrology lab: shadow TTP metrology engineers in setting up OPC calibration measurements and PCM electrical test
Receive TTP's inline monitoring strategy: understand at which process steps TTP measures, and what triggers a lot hold
Review TTP's measurement system capability (MSA/GR&R) records for reference: use as benchmark for Green Fab's tool qualification target

P1b.title: Metrology tool FAT — GR&R, accuracy & inter-tool matching baseline
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Execute GR&R study per AIAG MSA methodology at vendor site: 10 samples × 3 operators × 3 replicates — target GR&R < 10% of tolerance
Verify CD-SEM accuracy using NIST-traceable CD reference standards: confirm measurement bias within ±0.5 nm across measurement range
Verify film thickness accuracy using certified VLSI-Standards multilayer stacks: ellipsometry and XRF cross-check
Measure inter-tool offset between all same-type tools at vendor site: document systematic offsets for correction factors
Verify metrology data export format compatibility with MES/SPC system: test automated result upload in simulation

P1c.title: Metrology bay environmental specification & reference standard storage design
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Define metrology bay environmental specifications: floor vibration spectrum, temperature stability (±0.5°C for CD-SEM area), humidity, EMI
Specify vibration isolation pad requirements for CD-SEM and overlay metrology tools (active vs. passive isolation)
Design reference standard storage: temperature-controlled cabinet, N2 purge for oxide reference wafers, cleanroom class for wafer storage
Define metrology bay layout: minimize wafer transport time from process tools to metrology tools to limit surface change during transport
Specify electrical grounding and EMI shielding requirements for electron beam metrology tools

P2.title: Silicon measurement campaign, SPC deployment & model calibration data delivery
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Execute full PCM measurement matrix on test vehicle wafers: CD-SEM all critical layers, overlay all layer pairs, film thickness all deposition steps, sheet resistance all implant layers
Provide calibrated WAT/PCM dataset to N3 (PDK team) for SPICE model calibration: document measurement uncertainty for each parameter
Execute OPC CD-SEM measurement campaign: measure OPC calibration targets (dense/isolated lines, 2D patterns) on each litho lot
Establish SPC control charts for all critical inline parameters: set control limits from P2 lot statistical data, configure MES → SPC pipeline
Validate inline-to-yield correlation: identify which inline parameters (CD, overlay, film thickness) have highest statistical correlation with final electrical yield

P3.title: HVM metrology optimization, virtual metrology & sampling plan tuning
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Optimize metrology sampling plan: reduce frequency on well-controlled parameters; increase on high-variability, high-yield-impact parameters
Deploy virtual metrology models in production R2R APC control loop: validate VM prediction accuracy < 2× measurement precision before deployment
Implement advanced CD-SEM techniques: scatterometry (OCD) for CD/profile measurement on select layers to reduce cycle time
Conduct annual metrology tool re-qualification: full GR&R on production representative wafers; compare to P2 baseline; recalibrate if degraded
Develop measurement capability roadmap for next technology node: identify metrology gaps for sub-22nm process monitoring

P0.title: QMS framework setup, quality targets & reliability test plan definition
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Draft QMS framework: quality policy, quality objectives, risk-based approach (ISO 9001:2015), document control system (DCS)
Define quality acceptance criteria for each phase: P2 exit criteria (yield ≥ 60%, CPk baseline, FMEA complete), P3 exit criteria (yield ≥ 80%, reliability passed, PQE sign-off)
Review TTP's transferred reliability models (TDDB, BTI, HCI, EM) and assess completeness against JEDEC JEP001 requirements
Define reliability qualification plan: HTOL, HAST, TC test flows, sample sizes, vehicle design, milestone schedule aligned with P2/P3 timeline
Define FMEA scope: process-level FMEA for all critical modules during P2; update to HVM FMEA in P3

P1a.title: Reliability model audit, HTOL/HAST/TC test flow review & FMEA methodology training
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive and audit TTP's reliability model package: TDDB, BTI, HCI, EM lifetime models — verify activation energies, acceleration factors, and model forms match JEDEC JEP001 requirements
Audit HTOL/HAST/TC test flow documentation: verify stress conditions (125°C/1000hr HTOL; 130°C/85%RH/96hr HAST; -65°C to +150°C/1000 cycles TC) match JEDEC JESD22 standards
Receive and audit FA flow decision tree: verify coverage of top process failure mechanisms for 28nm CMOS
Conduct training at TTP reliability lab: observe HTOL/HAST test setup, understand test vehicle design, review FA methodology
Audit TTP's sample size and FIT rate calculation methodology: verify consistency with JEDEC JESD85

P1b.title: Equipment vendor quality documentation review & reliability specs for tool acceptance
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Review equipment vendor ISO 9001 quality documentation and MTBF/MTTR reliability data from installed base — compare to SEMI E10 targets
Define reliability acceptance criteria in FAT/SAT protocols: minimum MTBF requirement, MTTR target, availability specification for critical tools
Establish incoming inspection criteria for materials and chemicals: purity specifications, certificate of analysis requirements, supplier qualification protocol
Review equipment vendor service contract terms: on-call FSE response time commitment, spare parts availability, software update policy
Build supplier qualification database: track vendor quality performance (on-time delivery, non-conformance rate) from P0 through production

P1c.title: QMS documentation system setup, EHS framework & construction quality audit
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Deploy DMS (Document Management System): document taxonomy, numbering system, revision control, distribution, and retention policy — integrate with N7's IT infrastructure
Establish EHS compliance framework: chemical handling procedures, emergency response plans, waste disposal protocols — aligned with Vietnamese environmental law
Conduct construction phase quality audit: verify construction quality against design specifications, track non-conformances, issue punch-list
Set up corrective action tracking system (CAPA): document, track, and verify closure of all non-conformances identified during construction
Define equipment calibration and maintenance record management in CMMS — aligned with QMS document control requirements

P2.title: Process FMEA, defect playbook, reliability test initiation & first quality audit
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Execute process FMEA for all critical modules: identify failure modes, effects, severity (S), occurrence (O), detection (D) ratings — address all high-RPN items
Build defect playbook from P2 yield learning: classify top-10 defect modes by root cause, morphology, spatial signature — document corrective actions
Initiate HTOL reliability test lots from P2 silicon: stress at 125°C with Vdd bias — target 1000 hr readout per JESD22-A108F
Initiate HAST test lots: 130°C/85%RH biased — target 96 hr readout per JESD22-A110E
Conduct first internal quality audit of process bring-up: verify procedure compliance, SPC chart completeness, training records — issue non-conformance report

P3.title: Reliability qualification completion, FIT rate calculation, CoC & customer PQE sign-off
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Complete HTOL 1000 hr readout: analyze time-to-fail using Weibull/lognormal statistics; calculate FIT rate with 60% confidence interval per JESD85
Complete HAST and Temperature Cycling readouts: confirm 0 failures — zero-failure qualification per JEDEC JEP001-3A
Prepare qualification report: stress test results, FA of any failures, FIT rate summary, acceleration factor derivation, DPPM estimate
Conduct customer PQE review meeting: present qualification data; obtain written acceptance signature from customer PQE
Issue Certificate of Conformance (CoC) for first production lots; establish ongoing reliability monitoring (quarterly HTOL sampling from production)

P0.title: DMS setup, IT/OT architecture design & MES platform selection
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Deploy DMS: select platform (SharePoint/Confluence/custom), define document taxonomy, access control (RBAC based on NDA and security clearance), audit trail — operational before P1a begins
Design IT/OT network segmentation: enterprise IT (Level 3-4) vs. manufacturing OT (Level 0-2) per ISA/IEC 62443 Purdue model; define DMZ and firewall rules
Select MES platform: evaluate Camstar/Opcenter, SAP Digital Manufacturing, or custom — define deployment architecture and implementation timeline
Define SECS/GEM host architecture: E30/E37/E87/E90/E94 coverage, EAP design, DCV master list structure — use TTP's transferred SECS/GEM configuration as reference
Establish cybersecurity policy: RBAC, patch management, incident response plan, VPN access for TTP during remote training

P1a.title: Receive IT/automation IP deliverables & DMS document ingestion
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Receive TTP's SECS/GEM configuration templates: DCV master list, event list, alarm list per tool type — load into DMS and audit completeness against agreed IP scope
Receive TTP's FDC rule framework: documented fault signatures, threshold guidance per tool category — load into FDC system development environment
Set up DMS document ingestion workflow: tag all received documents by IP category, classification level, and receiving engineer
Track deliverable completeness: maintain IP receipt log against TTP's committed delivery schedule; escalate overdue T1 items to project manager
Configure DMS access for TTP's remote technical support team: time-limited VPN access, audit logged, RBAC restricted to relevant document categories

P1b.title: SECS/GEM interface testing, MES tool registration & network commissioning
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Test SECS/GEM interface at vendor FAT site: verify E30/E37/E87/E90/E94 compliance using simulated MES host; document DCV completeness
After SAT: register each tool in MES — configure tool ID, location, state machine, route step assignment, DCV collection plan
Configure SECS/GEM host connection for each installed tool: IP address, port, heartbeat, connection recovery logic
Commission IT/OT network: install OT network switches, configure firewall rules between IT and OT zones, test DMZ data flow
Deploy baseline FDC data collection for each tool: verify process data streaming to FDC server, configure initial alarm rules from TTP's framework

P1c.title: Server room construction coordination, IT infrastructure deployment & network cabling
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Define server room specifications: rack layout, power capacity (UPS), cooling (N+1 redundancy), physical security access control
Coordinate with N8 (Facility) on server room construction: floor loading, electrical, HVAC, cable management
Deploy core server infrastructure: MES application servers, FDC servers, data historian, DMS servers — configure RAID, backup, monitoring
Install and commission IT/OT network infrastructure: OT switches, industrial firewalls, CCTV, interlock integration points
Deploy MES platform: install, configure, and test MES application before tool registration begins in P1b

P2.title: APC/SPC/FDC commissioning, YMS deployment & data analytics for yield learning
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Commission R2R APC models for critical steps: litho CD control, etch depth control, CMP removal rate control — validate model prediction accuracy using P2 lot data
Deploy production-grade FDC rules: tune thresholds using P2 tool data to minimize false alarms while maintaining fault detection sensitivity
Activate SPC data pipeline: tool sensor → MES → SPC server; verify data latency < 5 min from measurement to chart update
Deploy YMS: import defect inspection data and correlate with WAT electrical yield — generate first defect Pareto and inline-to-yield correlation report
Configure automated WAT data extraction pipeline from electrical test to SPICE model calibration environment for N3 (PDK team)

P3.title: HVM MES optimization, cybersecurity hardening, MLOps & data governance
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Optimize MES dispatching for HVM throughput: model WIP flow, identify bottleneck tools, adjust priority rules and Q-time enforcement for HVM lot mix
Complete IEC 62443 cybersecurity hardening: patch all OT systems, remove default accounts, deploy endpoint detection on MES servers, conduct penetration test
Deploy MLOps pipeline: automate FDC model retraining, APC model update, virtual metrology model deployment — with model performance monitoring
Establish data governance: define data ownership, access control, retention periods (5 years minimum for JEDEC compliance), archival format
Implement 99.9%+ MES/DMS system availability target: configure N+1 redundancy, automated failover, backup verification

P0.title: Site feasibility, construction scope definition & utility system design
P0.description: Support the preparation phase: review TTP proposal, define IP scope, prepare DMS and contract terms. Specific task allocation per engineer group — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Conduct site feasibility study: evaluate candidate sites for floor vibration spectrum (critical for litho tools), proximity to chemical and gas supply, water availability for UPW system, electrical grid capacity
Assess environmental regulatory requirements at each candidate site: air emissions permits, chemical waste discharge permits, Vietnamese EHS law compliance
Develop cleanroom design specification: target class (ISO 3 for litho bays, ISO 4-5 general fab), airflow volume, HVAC architecture, vibration isolation approach
Design utility system scope: UPW plant capacity (liters/day), bulk gas supply system, specialty gas manifolds, chemical distribution, power/UPS, abatement system
Select and contract architect (cleanroom design specialist) and general contractor; issue construction RFP with utility specifications

P1a.title: Not primary stream — Construction oversight support & facility readiness coordination
P1a.description: Audit and manage deliverables from TTP; co-develop IP/test vehicle; training at TTP's fab. IMEC paper (read): R1 co-owned IP; bilateral contract required before knowledge sharing. Per Micron TTLA (read): process info + design info + preproduction wafers + transfer sessions. Specific audit formats, training session structures — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Review TTP's utility specification requirements in transferred technology documents: confirm construction design meets TTP's transferred process utility needs
Coordinate with N1 (Process) and N4 (Equipment) on any process utility requirements identified during P1a technology review that affect P1c construction plan
Track construction readiness milestones against the P1b equipment delivery schedule: ensure cleanroom is ready when tools arrive
Provide construction status updates to project steering committee: alert if construction delays will push tool installation outside planned window
Ensure DMS has received all construction-related documents: design drawings, permit applications, utility specifications

P1b.title: Utility commissioning at tools, cleanroom qualification & abatement verification
P1b.description: PO to vendors, co-develop specs/recipes, training at vendor site, FAT, deliver, hook-up, SAT. Tower/Toshiba case (read): equipment and technology transfer validation ran in parallel. Specific FAT/SAT protocols, SECS/GEM details — training knowledge; SEMI standards cited but not accessed.
key_tasks: Commission utility connections at each installed tool: verify UPW resistivity at load port (≥ 18.2 MΩ·cm), gas purity at chamber inlet, chemical concentration at dispense point, exhaust flow rate at duct connection
Conduct cleanroom particle count certification under full operational load (all tools powered on, full personnel count): verify ISO class maintained
Test abatement system under maximum estimated process load: verify destruction/removal efficiency ≥ 99.999% for PFC gases per EPA standard
Verify chemical waste collection and treatment at each wet tool: measure flow rate, test neutralization, verify waste tank level alarms
Commission HVAC final balance: verify airflow volume, uniformity, and temperature/humidity stability across all cleanroom zones with tools installed

P1c.title: Cleanroom construction lead, utility installation & Facility Readiness Test (FRT)
P1c.description: Feasibility study, design, construct fab, Facility Readiness Test. ISO 14644-1 is a real standard; specific specs (UPW quality, airflow) are training knowledge.
key_tasks: Manage cleanroom shell construction: structural review, HVAC installation, FFU (Fan Filter Unit) installation, raised floor, sub-fab layout
Commission UPW system: achieve resistivity ≥ 18.2 MΩ·cm, TOC < 1 ppb, bacteria < 0.001 CFU/mL, particles < 1/mL at 0.05 µm
Commission bulk gas systems (N2, O2, Ar, H2) and specialty gas systems (SiH4, NF3, Cl2, HBr, NH3): verify purity at point-of-use; install leak detection and purifiers
Commission chemical distribution systems: HF, H2O2, TMAH, NH4OH, IPA delivery — test N2 blanket, leak detection, waste neutralization
Conduct Facility Readiness Test (FRT): ISO 14644-1 particle count certification at all cleanroom zones; airflow and temperature uniformity; all utility systems at spec — sign FRT certificate

P2.title: Utility stability support for test vehicle runs & performance optimization
P2.description: Test vehicle run, process qualification, PDK v0.9 build and release to customer, yield learning, defect playbook, FMEA — target yield = 60%. JEDEC JEP001 (web search snippet): minimum PCM measurement set. ITRS (snippet): P2 = 'Yield Ramp' phase. Specific SPICE parameters, DRC/LVS tool details — training knowledge.
key_tasks: Provide 24/7 utility monitoring during test vehicle wafer campaign: real-time dashboard for UPW quality, gas purity, chemical concentration, HVAC parameters
Respond to utility excursions within 15 minutes for parameters affecting in-process wafers: document incident, corrective action, and wafer impact correlation
Optimize UPW recirculation system based on P2 actual consumption: minimize reject water rate while maintaining quality (target < 10% reject-to-produce ratio)
Analyze utility consumption patterns from P2: update capacity model for P3 HVM planning — identify any systems needing capacity upgrade
Establish utility PM schedule: define PM intervals for UPW membranes, gas purifiers, abatement catalysts, HVAC filters based on P2 operational data

P3.title: HVM utility capacity verification, sustainability program & long-term operations
P3.description: Achieve yield = 80% on customer design; complete all documents and knowhow; qualify PDK v1.0. JEDEC JESD22 reliability tests (HTOL/HAST/TC) exist — specific stress conditions from training knowledge.
key_tasks: Verify utility capacity meets HVM wafer start targets: model UPW, gas, and chemical demand at full committed capacity; confirm all systems have minimum 20% headroom
Implement energy efficiency program: variable-speed drives on HVAC fans, waste heat recovery, LED lighting, intelligent HVAC scheduling — target energy intensity reduction per wafer
Implement water reduction program: maximize UPW recirculation, implement rinse water recovery from wet tools — track and report liters/wafer consumed
Operate utilities with 99.9%+ availability target: implement N+1 redundancy for UPW pumps, gas manifold switchover, UPS
Plan capacity expansion utility upgrades: identify systems requiring expansion for next capacity step; budget capital investment; negotiate extended supply contracts
